text.title: Trajectory Eval Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns in agent evaluation pipelines. Each pairs symptom + root cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Eval suite reports "% correct" and nothing else. Cost regressions after model swap go unnoticed until the monthly bill.

root-cause.source: Internal — Sonnet 4.5→4.6 case; common pattern in startup eval kits

antipattern.root-cause: Author treated eval as a unit test and used the simplest pass/fail metric. Multi-axis cost surface was invisible.
antipattern.fix: Add `trajectory` (steps + loops) and `resources` (tokens + cost + latency) scorers. Gate PRs on all three.

antipattern.symptom: Spans carry vendor-specific attribute names (`anthropic_input_tokens`, `oai_completion_cost`). Cross-vendor eval requires custom mapping per backend.

root-cause.source: OpenTelemetry semconv drift; pre-1.0 GenAI conventions

antipattern.root-cause: Provider SDK added its own attribute set before OTel standardized GenAI. Author shipped without checking semconv.
antipattern.fix: Standardize on `gen_ai.*` GenAI semconv attribute names; map provider-specific extras into `gen_ai.system`-prefixed namespace.

antipattern.symptom: Each LLM call is a span, but tool calls are not. Debug session shows the model called something but no info on the tool outcome.
antipattern.root-cause: Author instrumented the SDK boundary but not the application's tool dispatcher.
antipattern.fix: Wrap every tool dispatcher in `tracer.start_as_current_span("agent.tool.<name>")` with args_hash + outcome + duration_ms.

antipattern.symptom: OTLP payloads exceed 1MB on long agent runs. Backend rate-limits trace ingestion; PII appears in trace UIs.

root-cause.source: OTLP payload-size guidance; internal trace-store cost audits

antipattern.root-cause: Author stored full prompt text in `gen_ai.prompt.0.content` attribute; long-loop traces became multi-megabyte.
antipattern.fix: Hash prompts (SHA-256) → store hash in span; keep raw text in side store keyed by hash. Apply PII redaction at the hash boundary.
